goal: Define the document-only handoff boundary from Builder Task Contract Candidate output to a future Human Gate Review Candidate without approving, creating, or executing Builder tasks.
claims: BP-052 defines only a document-level handoff from Builder Task Candidate output to a future Human Gate Review Candidate.
BP-052 keeps approval, task creation, execution, and live Builder calls blocked.
BP-052 does not implement UI, auth, secrets, DB, persistence, approval action, or Builder integration.
assumptions: Builder Task Candidate output is the required source for later Human Gate review input.
A human-review envelope is needed before any future task creation decision.
Live Builder task creation remains blocked until a separate readiness and approval task explicitly allows it.
dod: The document defines required input, output, gates, blocked behavior, and non-goals.
The document explicitly prevents automatic approval, Task Create, Execute, Approve, Push, Deploy, auth, secrets, DB, and live Builder calls.
stop_conditions: Runtime, fixture, package, or tool changes become necessary
Live Builder, auth, secrets, DB, deploy, execution, task creation, approve, or push becomes necessary
prior_task_findings: BP-051 made Builder Task Candidate fixture coverage visible and identified Human Gate Handoff as the next safe boundary.
